unit.note: F0 fresh gate green. Server vitest 166 files / 1924 passed + 1 skipped (1925) (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local) + tsc --noEmit clean + npm run build green. New F18 coverage lives in the scripts node:test suite (node --test scripts/install-windows.test.mjs): 5 passed (3 pre-existing npm-gating contracts + 2 new): (1) the VBS launcher is written with -Encoding Unicode and NEVER -Encoding ASCII (ASCII maps char>127 to '?', corrupting a non-ASCII repo path); (2) after writing, the installer re-reads $VbsPath (Get-Content) and can abort (exit 1) if the embedded server path did not round-trip. AC2 proven RED-first on pre-fix main (2/2 new failed: -Encoding ASCII present + no Get-Content re-read after the write), green after. The scripts suite is separate from the vitest workspaces (root scripts/, run via node --test), so its 5 are reported here in the note rather than folded into the 1924 vitest count. Client workspace NOT re-run: no client/** file changed (detect_frontend_changes.py has_frontend_changes=false); the change is confined to scripts/install-windows.ps1 + its co-located test, outside both workspaces.

integration.note: Confidence-calibration probes of the installer->VBS-file->wscript boundary (producer = install-windows.ps1 stamping $ServerDir; consumer = wscript.exe / node reading the embedded path), run REAL on this Win11 box. (1) Round-trip probe: an umlaut path C:\Users\Müller\...\server written with -Encoding Unicode, re-read with Get-Content -Raw -Encoding Unicode -> .Contains($escapedServerDir) TRUE (path survives); no finding. (2) Negative/corruption probe: the SAME content written with the OLD -Encoding ASCII -> re-read .Contains() FALSE (umlaut became '?') -> proves the round-trip guard is NOT vacuous and actually catches F18. (3) BOM probe: the -Encoding Unicode file's first two bytes are FF FE (UTF-16LE BOM, the documented WSH read signal) -> wscript reads it natively; no finding. (4) Refactor-preservation probe: the new $escapedServerDir variable form is byte-identical to the old inline $($ServerDir -replace '\\','\\') form; no finding. Two-plus consecutive clean rounds => asymptote reached, boundary calibrated. Edge not probed: a path containing '"' - accepted, unreachable ('"' is an illegal Windows filename char; $ServerDir is a real directory).

e2e.note: No Playwright run. This iterate touches ONLY scripts/install-windows.ps1 (the Windows autostart installer) + its co-located node:test file scripts/install-windows.test.mjs - no client/src or server/src file, no DOM/route/component, no Vite-config/proxy/WS-origin/CORS surface. detect_frontend_changes.py reports has_frontend_changes=false (nothing under client/**). F0.5 surface=none (see surface_verification); a browser smoke exercises nothing this installer-encoding change affects.

surface_verification.justification: This iterate changes only scripts/install-windows.ps1 (the Windows login-autostart installer that writes the start-server.vbs launcher) + its co-located node:test. No file under client/src or server/src is touched - detect_frontend_changes.py reports has_frontend_changes=false, so there is genuinely no browser/DOM/route/Vite-config/proxy/WS/CORS surface to verify. The behavior (the VBS must be written as UTF-16LE so a non-ASCII repo path survives, and the install must abort loudly if it does not) is only observable by writing + re-reading the launcher file under PowerShell, which the structural node:test pins (encoding flag + round-trip guard presence, AC2 RED-first on pre-fix main: -Encoding ASCII + no re-read -> 2/2 failed, green after) and the empirical on-Windows PowerShell smoke reproduces (ASCII corrupts the umlaut path to '?', Unicode round-trips, UTF-16LE BOM present). F0.5 surface=none - no fabricated browser run.

behaviors.behavior: AC1 - install-windows.ps1 writes the autostart VBS as UTF-16LE (-Encoding Unicode) instead of ASCII, so a non-ASCII repo path (umlaut/accent/CJK, e.g. C:\Users\Müller\...) is no longer corrupted to '?', and a post-write round-trip check aborts the install loudly if the embedded path did not survive.
behaviors.evidence: install-windows.test.mjs: the Set-Content writing $VbsPath uses -Encoding Unicode and NOT -Encoding ASCII; after the write the script re-reads $VbsPath (Get-Content) and can exit 1. Empirical PowerShell smoke on this Win11 box: umlaut path written Unicode round-trips TRUE, written ASCII round-trips FALSE (corrupted to '?'), UTF-16LE BOM FF FE present.

behaviors.behavior: AC2 - a NEW regression test is RED on pre-fix main and GREEN after.
behaviors.evidence: RED captured on pre-fix main (line 116 was -Encoding ASCII, no post-write re-read): 2/2 new tests failed - the Unicode-not-ASCII assertion failed on the literal ASCII line, and the round-trip-check assertion failed for the missing Get-Content re-read of $VbsPath. GREEN after the fix: node --test scripts/install-windows.test.mjs -> 5 passed / 0 failed.

behaviors.behavior: AC3 - full server suite + build stay green; no invariant/ADR regressed (the 3 pre-existing npm exit-code-gating + no-stderr-to-$null contracts stay green).
behaviors.evidence: Server vitest 166 files / 1924 passed + 1 skipped (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local) + tsc + npm run build green; the 3 pre-existing install-windows.test.mjs contracts stay green. Client not re-run (no client/** change). Read-only-observer rules 1/12 intact (the installer writes only into %USERPROFILE%\.shipwright-webui\ + the Startup folder; no ~/.claude/projects/** or shipwright_run_config.json).

behaviors.behavior: AC4 - files stay within the expected footprint; bloat baseline not ratcheted.
behaviors.evidence: Exact footprint: scripts/install-windows.ps1 (136 -> 152) + scripts/install-windows.test.mjs (58 -> 110, the AC2-mandated co-located regression test) - both < 300, neither is in shipwright_bloat_baseline.json (no new crossing, baseline unchanged). Serial #13, deps/blocks none, D01-D12 merged -> no live parallel collision.
